title: Delicate Smoky Japanese Tortilla Wrap
description: A satvik-friendly Japanese dish featuring a flour tortilla with smoky and floral flavors, inspired by the Kyushu region.
instructions: Preheat a large skillet or griddle over medium heat.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame oil.
Brush the mixture onto the flour tortilla, making sure to coat it evenly.
Add the smoked tofu to the skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, until lightly browned.
Add the pickled plum, shiso leaves, sesame seeds, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Place a cooked tortilla on a flat surface and spoon the tofu and plum mixture onto the center of the tortilla.
Fold the bottom half of the tortilla up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the tortilla into a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients and serve immediately.
